visa_free.notes: Kenya and Eritrea permanently abolished visa requirements for their citizens, announced by Kenyan President Ruto on February 9, 2023. The term 'permanently' is used; no specific maximum stay or conditions were published in the official announcement. Reciprocal ordinary-passport bilateral waiver. As of 2024, Kenya's Immigration also waived ETA fees for Eritreans, confirming the arrangement remains in effect.

visa_policy.notes: Official Eritrean embassy/consulate sources (Washington D.C., Melbourne AU, London UK) state that a visa is required for all non-Eritreans and for Eritreans without a valid National ID. No official Eritrean government website publishes a comprehensive list of visa-exempt nationalities. The 1992 Regulation No. 4/1992 on Travel Documents and Immigration establishes the visa framework. The Kenya-Eritrea mutual visa waiver was confirmed on Kenya's official State House website (president.go.ke) on February 9, 2023 as a permanent abolition of visa requirements for both countries' citizens; no specific max stay was published. Uganda visa-free entry for Ugandan nationals into Eritrea is reported by aggregators (TIMATIC/Wikipedia) but not confirmed from any official Eritrean or Ugandan government source specifically covering Eritrea's admission policy — omitted per research policy. Ethiopia and Sudan visa-on-arrival for Eritrea is reported only via TIMATIC/Wikipedia aggregators — not confirmed from any official source — omitted. Eritrean nationals holding a valid National ID do not require a visa to enter Eritrea. Children of Eritrean parentage under 18 holding an Eritrean under-age ID card may also travel without a visa (confirmed by UK Embassy consular services page). The e-visa portal evisa.mfa.gov.er returned ECONNREFUSED at time of research. Eritrea withdrew from IGAD on December 12, 2025 (confirmed via shabait.com). COMESA Free Movement Protocol signed only by Burundi, Kenya, Rwanda, and Zimbabwe — not Eritrea.

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program found on any official Eritrean government source. No mention of CBI on Eritrean embassy/consulate websites.

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of diplomatic passports, service passports, and passports for public affairs issued by China are visa-exempt for up to 30 days per entry. Based on the bilateral mutual visa exemption agreement between the People's Republic of China and Eritrea, entered into force 2015-04-15. Individuals intending to stay beyond 30 days must complete residence formalities with local authorities.

validation_notes: ADDED: Kenya (KEN) to visa_free based on official Kenya State House announcement (president.go.ke, Feb 9 2023) confirming permanent abolition of visa requirements for both countries' citizens — this was previously omitted despite having an official-government-side source (Kenya's own State House website).

REMOVED: immigration.go.ug from official_domains — it was listed as an official source for Eritrea's policy toward Ugandans, but that page reflects Uganda's inbound visa policy (who Uganda admits), not Eritrea's outbound/inbound visa policy toward Ugandans. Source mismatch corrected.
VERIFIED: China conditional_access entry (diplomatic/service/official passports, 30 days, in force 2015-04-15) — confirmed unchanged against cs.mfa.gov.cn.
VERIFIED: No e-visa portal accessible (evisa.mfa.gov.er returns ECONNREFUSED on re-check).

VERIFIED: Libya diplomatic/service passport exemption (claimed 60 days by aggregators) — Libya MFA (foreign.gov.ly) has no mention of Eritrea exemption; only aggregator/Wikipedia sources cite this — NOT added per research policy requiring official-source confirmation.
VERIFIED: Uganda visa-free for Ugandans entering Eritrea — only aggregator sources; no official Eritrean or Ugandan government source confirmed Eritrea's admission policy toward Ugandans — NOT added.
VERIFIED: Ethiopia/Sudan visa on arrival — only TIMATIC/Wikipedia aggregators; no official source — NOT added.
VERIFIED: Eritrea withdrawn from IGAD December 12, 2025 per shabait.com.
VERIFIED: COMESA Free Movement Protocol — Eritrea not a signatory per comesa.int.
gaps: No publicly accessible official Eritrean government immigration portal found (evisa.mfa.gov.er returns ECONNREFUSED; no mfa.gov.er or immigration.gov.er accessible).

Kenya-Eritrea visa waiver (Feb 9, 2023): confirmed as permanent bilateral waiver from Kenya's official State House site; however, no official maximum stay published in the announcement. No Eritrean government source independently confirms. Max stay therefore recorded as null.

Uganda visa-free entry for Ugandans into Eritrea is widely cited by aggregators (TIMATIC/Wikipedia) and Uganda's own immigration page lists Eritrea as visa-exempt for Ugandans traveling to Uganda (not vice versa). No Eritrean official source nor specific Ugandan official source confirms Eritrea's policy for Ugandan nationals — omitted.
Ethiopia and Sudan visa-on-arrival for Eritrea reported only via TIMATIC/Wikipedia aggregators — not confirmed from any official Eritrean government source — omitted.
Libya diplomatic/service passport visa exemption (reportedly 60 days) cited in aggregators but not found on Libya's MFA website (foreign.gov.ly) or any official source — omitted.
No official Eritrean government page in English lists specific bilateral visa agreements or exemptions for foreign nationals.
Eritrea's official government website shabait.com (Ministry of Information) does not publish immigration/visa policy details.
No data found on residency by investment, golden visa, or fast-track/skilled migrant schemes from any official source.

No credential-based conditional access rules found (no rules granting Eritrea entry based on holding a US visa, Schengen visa, UK visa, residence permit, or permanent residency in a third country) from any official Eritrean government source.
No OCI or similar special diaspora/heritage status rules applicable to Eritrea entry found from any official source.
Eritrea has not signed the COMESA Free Movement Protocol (per comesa.int); not a member of IGAD as of December 2025.

outbound_official.note: no official Eritrea government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data

sources_checked.description: Eritrean Embassy Washington D.C. — official embassy visa page; confirms visa required for all non-Eritreans
Consulate of the State of Eritrea in Melbourne, Australia — official visa services page (timed out on re-check; previously confirmed visa required for all non-Eritreans)
Eritrean Embassy UK London — consular services page; confirms visa required; Eritreans under 35 without national ID must apply like any non-national
UK FCDO travel advice — entry requirements for Eritrea; confirms all visitors must obtain a visa prior to entry; no visa-free exceptions listed
Official Website of the President of the Republic of Kenya — confirms bilateral permanent abolition of visa requirements for Kenyan and Eritrean citizens, announced February 9, 2023
Eritrea Regulation No. 4/1992 on Travel Documents and Immigration (UNHCR Refworld archive of official Eritrean legislation)
sources_checked.notes: Third-party archive of official Eritrean legislation; original Eritrean government source not directly accessible

sources_checked.description: Eritrea Ministry of Information — official press release confirming IGAD withdrawal on December 12, 2025
Purported Eritrean official e-visa portal — ECONNREFUSED on re-check; site not accessible
sources_checked.notes: Connection refused; site does not appear to exist at time of research

sources_checked.description: COMESA official website — Free Movement Protocol; confirms Eritrea has not signed/ratified the protocol

China Ministry of Foreign Affairs — official list of mutual visa exemption agreements; confirms China-Eritrea bilateral agreement for diplomatic, service, and public affairs passports, entered into force 2015-04-15, max stay 30 days
Serbia Ministry of Foreign Affairs — confirms diplomatic and official passport holders from Serbia require a visa for Eritrea, supporting bilateral-only nature of China exemption
Libya Ministry of Foreign Affairs — re-checked; no mention of Eritrea or visa exemption agreement found on the page

Wikipedia: Visa policy of Eritrea — aggregator source consulted to identify leads; cites Kenya/Uganda visa-free (ordinary), Ethiopia/Sudan visa on arrival, China/Libya diplomatic exemptions. Non-official; used only for leads.
